solution: 
```java
class Trie {
    private TrieNode root;

    public Trie() {
        root = new TrieNode();
    }

    public void insert(String word) {
        TrieNode node = root;
        for (char ch : word.toCharArray()) {
            node = node.children.computeIfAbsent(ch, c -> new TrieNode());
        }
        node.isEndOfWord = true;
    }

    public boolean search(String word) {
        TrieNode node = root;
        for (char ch : word.toCharArray()) {
            if (!node.children.containsKey(ch)) {
                return false;
            }
            node = node.children.get(ch);
        }
        return node.isEndOfWord;
    }

    public boolean startsWith(String prefix) {
        TrieNode node = root;
        for (char ch : prefix.toCharArray()) {
            if (!node.children.containsKey(ch)) {
                return false;
            }
            node = node.children.get(ch);
        }
        return true;
    }

    private static class TrieNode {
        Map<Character, TrieNode> children;
        boolean isEndOfWord;

        public TrieNode() {
            children = new HashMap<>();
            isEndOfWord = false;
        }
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(L) where L is the length of the word or prefix
space complexity: O(N * L) where N is the number of words and L is the average length of the words
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The Trie is implemented using a nested TrieNode class with a HashMap to store children. Insert, search, and startsWith operations traverse the Trie character by character, making them efficient with linear time complexity. The use of HashMap ensures flexibility and efficient lookups. The main weakness is higher memory usage due to HashMap overhead, but this is offset by the fast operations.
